title: paspalidium geminatum subsp. paludivagum
intro: Paspalidium geminatum subsp. paludivagum, also known as palo verde grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparation of Paspalidium geminatum subsp. paludivagum can be made by drying and grinding the leaves, roots, or stems, and can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or tincture.
